w:r.w:t: Momentum App: Technical & Design Governance Document (v5)
This document serves as the mandatory, authoritative governance for all Momentum source code and design implementation.
C. Mandatory Data Model: Unified Household Membership
This project mandates a 4-service "Backend for Frontend" (BFF) architecture.
. The Next.js web client and its embedded BFF.
. The new, standalone serverless API that serves the
is the mandated state library for its simplicity and minimal boilerplate.
is mandated for all server-state, caching, and mutations.
On launch, show the "Household / Profile Selection" screen (Family View).
If a child profile is selected, navigate to the Kiosk View.
If a parent profile is selected, show a secure auth screen (e.g., PIN/biometrics) before navigating to the Parent View.
Momentum App: Technical & Design Governance Document (v4)
This document serves as the mandatory, authoritative governance for all Momentum source code and design implementation.
C. Mandatory Data Model: Unified Household Membership
This new section is based on research into the "Streak Anxiety Paradox" and neurodivergent-affirming design.
All motivational mechanics must reward achievement, not punish failure. The tone must be supportive and non-judgmental.
Punitive mechanics (like breaking a streak) are a "primary source of user anxiety and app abandonment" for neurodivergent users (Source: Neurodivergent UX Research, 3.2).
Rigid "streak" counters that reset to zero on a single missed day.
This mechanic triggers "all or nothing" thinking and "heightens anxiety," which is counter-productive to the app's "Calm Signal" goal.
Use "gentle gamification" (e.g., points, confetti, "Winventory" (Done Lists), and positive feedback).
Provides the necessary dopamine/reward loop without the risk of failure-state anxiety.
If streaks are ever implemented (e.g., in Phase 4), they
This provides "relief" and acknowledges that "people will need breaks... without being punished for being human" (Source: Neurodivergent UX Research, 3.3).
Momentum App: Technical & Design Governance Document (v3)
This document serves as the mandatory, authoritative governance for all Momentum source code and design implementation.
A. General Code Principles (React, Node.js, JS/TS)
C. Mandatory Data Model: Unified Household Membership
This new architectural pattern is mandatory for modeling the relationship between FamilyMember and Household documents.
The previous model using two separate arrays (
schema MUST use a single, unified array named
This array will contain embedded sub-documents that store the member's profile
as it relates to that specific household
// This document stores the user's global identity and auth.
// This sub-document defines a member's role and status *within* this household.
familyMemberId: Types.ObjectId; // Reference to the global IFamilyMember
displayName: string; // Household-specific display name (e.g., "Papa Bear")
profileColor: string; // Household-specific color from the palette
pointsTotal: number; // Points earned *within* this household
// This is the main Household document.
// ... other household-level data (e.g., tasks, store items)
Momentum App: Technical & Design Governance Document (v2)

This document serves as the mandatory, authoritative governance for all Momentum source code and design implementation across Mobile (React Native), Web (React/Next.js), and API (Node.js/Express/Mongoose).
To enforce technical and design uniformity, ensuring maximum code velocity and minimum cognitive load in the final UI/UX.
Uniformity minimizes internal friction for the two-person team.
All new JavaScript/TypeScript for React Native (Mobile), React/Next.js (Web), and Node.js/Express (API).
Single language and style across the full stack.
Provides a widely-adopted, high-quality, community standard to build upon.
must be used for all modules. CI/CD must fail the build on any ESlint error.
Converts subjective stylistic preference into objective, mechanical non-compliance (Vibecoding Rule II).
A. General Code Principles (React, Node.js, JS/TS)
Mandatory for all Class, Interface, Type, and Component definitions.
Mandatory for all local variables, state variables, and object properties.
Mandatory for all function and method names. Must use verb-based naming.
Mandatory for global constants or static readonly values.
This is a specific extension to maintain clarity in the NoSQL data layer.
Mandatory singular form. Mongoose auto-pluralizes to the collection name.
Used for querying the database directly. All collection names must be lowercased plural nouns.
Mandatory. Consistent with surrounding JavaScript environment.
Must be clearly distinguishable from embedded properties.
The visual philosophy is "Calm Signal, Optional Spark."
. Navigation and essential actions use muted tones (color-text-secondary, color-border-subtle).
Low-friction interface. Reduces visual overwhelm and cognitive distraction.
Gamification elements (points, streaks, confetti) are controlled by the
toggle. These elements must use vibrant accent/signal colors (color-signal-success, color-signal-focus).
Provides necessary dopamine/reward loop only when requested by the user, respecting preference boundaries.
Child access is implemented via a
on the Family landing page. No login or password prompt is permitted for child task completion/store navigation.
High-velocity interaction; eliminates executive function barrier to task initiation.
The design must use semantic roles, implemented via CSS Variables or platform equivalent, to support future theming.
User Profile Colors (Mandatory Palette for Profiles - Total 10)
Must include a satisfying, brief micro-interaction (confetti, text strike-through, point counter slide-up). Total duration must be less than 400ms.
slow slides, bounces, or complex 3D transitions.
All motion must respect the system's
This section defines the mandatory, platform-specific setup.
(latest patch). Target Browser Baseline: Safari 16.4+, Chrome 111+, Firefox 128+ only.
Leveraging high-performance Rust engine; eliminates need for PostCSS plugins (autoprefixing, imports).
in the main CSS file to define all semantic color roles (e.g.,
Adheres to v4 best practices; simplifies configuration and ensures variables generate utility classes (e.g.,
Expo (SDK 54) + NativeWind v4.1.x + Tailwind CSS v3.4.x
. NativeWind v5 (for Tailwind v4) is a "pre-release" and is
to define theme and content paths.
Standard configuration path for NativeWind v4.
Required by NativeWind v4 to transform
Required by NativeWind v4 to process global CSS directives.
whenever possible. Use utility classes directly in the markup. When custom CSS is necessary, use native CSS variables (e.g.,
Follows Tailwind's core best practice and ensures maintainability.
be paired with a text label (e.g.,
Eliminates ambiguity and reduces cognitive load, a core ADHD-centric design principle.
(or system-default sans-serif fallback). Use the pre-defined hierarchy (H1:
Maximum legibility and clear visual hierarchy for structure.
This implements the required human checks for the two-person team.
Mandatory Pair Programming/PR Review: All features must be pair-programmed or subjected to a Pull Request review by the second developer.
Review Citation Rule: Code review feedback
cite the section number/rule name from
(e.g., "Violates Section 2.A: Constants must be UPPER_SNAKE_CASE") or a specific ESlint violation.
"Tech Debt Friday": 60 minutes must be allocated at the end of every sprint/week to address ESlint/TypeScript warnings and
Momentum App: Technical & Design Governance Document (v2)

This document serves as the mandatory, authoritative governance for all Momentum source code and design implementation across Mobile (React Native), Web (React/Next.js), and API (Node.js/Express/Mongoose).
To enforce technical and design uniformity, ensuring maximum code velocity and minimum cognitive load in the final UI/UX.
Uniformity minimizes internal friction for the two-person team.
All new JavaScript/TypeScript for React Native (Mobile), React/Next.js (Web), and Node.js/Express (API).
Single language and style across the full stack.
Provides a widely-adopted, high-quality, community standard to build upon.
must be used for all modules. CI/CD must fail the build on any ESlint error.
Converts subjective stylistic preference into objective, mechanical non-compliance (Vibecoding Rule II).
A. General Code Principles (React, Node.js, JS/TS)
Mandatory for all Class, Interface, Type, and Component definitions.
Mandatory for all local variables, state variables, and object properties.
Mandatory for all function and method names. Must use verb-based naming.
Mandatory for global constants or static readonly values.
This is a specific extension to maintain clarity in the NoSQL data layer.
Mandatory singular form. Mongoose auto-pluralizes to the collection name.
Used for querying the database directly. All collection names must be lowercased plural nouns.
Mandatory. Consistent with surrounding JavaScript environment.
Must be clearly distinguishable from embedded properties.
The visual philosophy is "Calm Signal, Optional Spark."
. Navigation and essential actions use muted tones (color-text-secondary, color-border-subtle).
Low-friction interface. Reduces visual overwhelm and cognitive distraction.
Gamification elements (points, streaks, confetti) are controlled by the
toggle. These elements must use vibrant accent/signal colors (color-signal-success, color-signal-focus).
Provides necessary dopamine/reward loop only when requested by the user, respecting preference boundaries.
Child access is implemented via a
on the Family landing page. No login or password prompt is permitted for child task completion/store navigation.
High-velocity interaction; eliminates executive function barrier to task initiation.
The design must use semantic roles, implemented via CSS Variables or platform equivalent, to support future theming.

Must include a satisfying, brief micro-interaction (confetti, text strike-through, point counter slide-up). Total duration must be less than 400ms.
slow slides, bounces, or complex 3D transitions.
